intro: Paepalanthus parvicephalus, also known as the small-headed Paepalanthus, is a plant species within the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related diseases. Bioactive constituents of Paepalanthus parvicephalus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Paepalanthus parvicephalus include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
